TopModule.demo_behavior:
Module: TopModule I would like you to implement a module named TopModule with the following interface. All input and output ports are one bit unless otherwise specified. - input  clock - input  a - output p - output q The module should implement a sequential circuit. Read the simulation waveforms to determine what the circuit does, then implement it. time   clock   a   p   q 0ns    0       0   x   x 5ns    0       0   x   x 10ns   0       0   x   x 15ns   0       0   x   x 20ns   0       0   x   x 25ns   1       0   0   x 30ns   1       0   0   x 35ns   1       0   0   x 40ns   1       0   0   x 45ns   1       0   0   x 50ns   1       0   0   x 55ns   0       0   0   0 60ns   0       0   0   0 65ns   0       0   0   0 70ns   0       1   0   0 75ns   0       0   0   0 80ns   0       1   0   0 85ns   1       0   0   0 90ns   1       1   1   0 95ns   1       0   0   0 100ns  1       1   1   0 105ns  1       0   0   0 110ns  1       1   1   0 115ns  0       0   1   1 120ns  0       1   1   1 125ns  0       0   1   1 130ns  0       1   1   1 135ns  0       0   1   1 140ns  0       0   1   1 145ns  1       0   0   1 150ns  1       0   0   1 155ns  1       0   0   1 160ns  1       0   0   1 165ns  1       1   1   1 170ns  1       0   0   1 175ns  0       1   0   0 180ns  0       0   0   0 185ns  0       1   0   0 190ns  0       0   0   0
Key rules: Implement behavior exactly as described in the prompt.
Reset: Only as explicitly described in the prompt.
Corner cases: No additional corner cases beyond prompt requirements.
Performance: Use prompt-defined timing/latency behavior.

test_goals: Pass benchmark harness checks for all provided tests.
verification.oracle_strategy: Use benchmark-provided reference outputs as oracle.
pass_fail_criteria: DUT outputs match oracle outputs for benchmark tests.
